free_text: The uncertainty in the photon energy is
estimated to be about 100 keV below 20 MeV
increasing to about 150 keV at 28 MeV.
The photon dose was measured using a thin-walled
transmission ion chamber the response of which was
intercalibrated against that of a standard replica
consisting of sixteen BF3 proportional gas counters
arranged on two concentric rings, each of eight tubes.
The neutron detection efficiency was determined from
measurements using calibrated neutron sources and a
measurement of the d(g,p)n reaction cross section.
37 independent yield curves were collected at
photon energies ranging from 10 to 26.5 MeV.
In the range from 10 to 19.5 Mev data were collected
at 100 keV and elsewhere at 200 keV intervals.
A total of 10 sample-out yield curves was measured.
Average yield curves, obtained from the two data sets,
allowed the yield curve for the 15N(g,xn) reaction

The contribution of the latter reaction, which was
studied earlier by measuring the yield of
annihilation gammas from the induced 13N positron
activity, was subtracted from the cross section data.
